shape_decode: cmp=W, vp=W, vpc=_, naive=L — cmplog AND value_profile each independently resolve the gate; naive blocks. A parallel-solvable gate where I2S substitution and VP Hamming/structural-gradient are redundant routes to the SAME target the branch needs.
round_note: FINAL round (loop-until-dry). 7 of 9 branches confirmed-frozen across rounds 1-2 (6x parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate + 1x parallel_hamming_gradient_value_closure/bloaty_57). 2 branches remain rule_not_met after their genuinely-different pivots were scored against the arbiter cache and did not hold: sqlite3_14563 (depth_reach H4 + joint_necessity H2 + the two parallel attempts = 3/4 rules scored, none held) and libxml2_6785 (4/4 rules scored, none held). This round adds NO new relaxed-threshold re-test of a failed measurement; it honestly resolves both remaining branches to decidable:false (G3), with the kept superset re-validating the 7 confirmed branches.
label_source: Layer-1 resolve pattern (cmp W, vp W, naive L). The evidence test is the INDEPENDENT corpus-content verification (G2): confirm BOTH resolving fuzzers' corpora carry / reach the target the gate needs, WITHOUT consulting the resolve pattern itself.

confirmed_frozen.note: These 7 branches were confirmed in rounds 1-2 (6 libpng fixed-offset literal/enum branches under operand_enrichment; bloaty_57 under value_distance_reached / Hamming closure). Their hypotheses are carried forward VERBATIM (superset/monotonic) so they re-validate. Not re-tested, relabeled, or dropped here.

shape_state.round3_disposition: Both remaining branches resolve to decidable:false. (1) sqlite3_14563: the cards describe a per-seed accumulation/iteration mechanism (codeInteger true branch taken ~32x per winning seed; densely operator-joined integer-literal body). The TWO independent built measurements able to test that mechanism BOTH refuted it on campaign data: joint_necessity (seed size + token count) clipped at the small-n token noise floor, and depth_reach (per-seed-normalized execution count at the branch line via the cov image) did not lift. The only mechanistically-adjacent untried tool is token_count, whose token arm joint_necessity already exercised and which would be a disguised relaxed re-test of the same accumulation claim (forbidden); corpus_size_ratio measures corpus-population growth, not the per-seed structural property the mechanism asserts, so it does not fit. No GENUINELY DIFFERENT discriminable mechanism remains after two independent accumulation measures refuted. Honest decidable:false per G3 — no mechanism invented to force a label. (2) libxml2_6785: unchanged gate-SHAPE limitation (moving parse cursor + single all-zero 1-byte operand) — no built tool can discriminate the parallel-NUL mechanism from coincidence.

hypotheses.label: A fixed-offset 1-4 byte VALUE literal/enum gates the branch; both I2S (direct substitution) and VP (Hamming/prefix gradient) drive the gate window toward the target value AND away from the decoy, so BOTH resolving corpora are enriched in the target and depleted in the decoy at the gate offset relative to naive.

params.note: Score the cmplog arm (signed_target_enrich/decoy_enrich, cmplog-vs-naive OE_CACHE) AND the value_profile arm (vp_signed_target_enrich/vp_decoy_enrich, vp-vs-naive OE_VP_CACHE) independently against naive. Per-branch gate offset+target/decoy bytes from the signature: libpng_3864/3951/4085 IHDR color_type offset 0x19 target 0x03 (decoy 0x00/0x04/0x06); libpng_3811/3890 chunk-type offset target 'sPLT' 73 50 4c 54 (decoy other FOURCC); libpng_3818 chunk-validation window.

hypotheses.prediction: Each resolving fuzzer's corpus drives the gate window toward the target literal: EITHER it over-represents the target byte vs naive (signed_target_enrich > 0.5) OR it depletes the decoy byte vs naive (decoy_enrich < -1.0) — the two are complementary corpus-content footprints of the same drive (target enrichment saturates low when naive's baseline target fraction is already high, as at the libpng chunk-type window; decoy depletion is then the cleaner signal). DISCRIMINATING: a structural/length gate returns no_gate_signature; a single-technique-only gate drives the window for ONLY ONE of {cmplog, value_profile}; a non-causal coincidence drives neither. Only the parallel-literal mechanism makes BOTH arms drive the SAME gate window.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment is computed over saved-corpus byte frequencies at the gate offset; it never reads the per-trial resolve/block label that defined the shape.

hypotheses.label: The gate is NOT a single fixed-offset literal but a structural/length/accumulation condition (repeated integer-token body, or a multi-comparison validation cascade). Both I2S and VP help by accumulating target tokens / growing the seed, so the resolving seeds are LARGER and carry MORE target tokens than the blocking seeds — there is no single alignable gate byte.

params.note: KEPT VERBATIM from prior round (superset). Aim joint_necessity at the conservative VP-resolving vs naive-blocking arm. Retained so the prior structural footprint stays measured; against the arbiter cache this rule did NOT hold for sqlite3_14563 (tag_lift clipped at the n_W noise floor), and depth_reach (the per-seed iteration angle, see decidable_false notes) also did not lift — so sqlite3_14563 now resolves to decidable:false. The hypothesis is kept VERBATIM only to satisfy the superset/monotonic invariant; it labels no branch this round.

hypotheses.prediction: size_lift >= 1.5 (the value_profile-resolving seeds are materially larger than naive-blocking — structural accumulation, not a single-byte flip) AND token_lift >= 1.2 (they carry more integer-literal/operator tokens). DISCRIMINATING: a fixed-offset literal gate would show size_lift ~ 1.0 and be caught by parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate's enrichment instead; absence of size/token lift means neither technique is accumulating structure.
hypotheses.g2_independence: Seed size + token counts are intrinsic corpus-content properties of the resolving vs blocking seed sets; they do not consult the resolve label.

hypotheses.label: NEW (confirmed round 2). A fixed-width VALUE operand (a 2-byte enum constant) gates the branch, but the discriminating corpus-content signal is not byte-frequency at one offset — it is HAMMING-DISTANCE CLOSURE: both resolving arms produce seeds whose closest sliding-window value lands at or near the exact gate operand, while naive's seeds stall far from it. Both I2S (lands the operand exactly) and VP (CMP_MAP climbs a Hamming gradient toward it) close the distance; naive cannot.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_prior: Genuinely different from parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate: that hypothesis used corpus_queue:byte_freq_ratio (fraction of corpus carrying the exact target byte at a FIXED offset) and could not score bloaty (VP arm no_gate_signature / cmplog enrichment alone insufficient). This uses seed_set:value_distance_reached (per-arm MIN Hamming distance to the operand over sliding windows) — a different source, compute, and discriminator. It rules out the alternative 'cmplog plants the byte but VP does NOT actually approach the operand': if VP's seeds do not land closer to 0x003e than naive's, the parallel-gradient claim is refuted.

params.note: EM_X86_64 = 0x003e, the 2-byte little-endian e_machine constant from the switch at elf.cc:782 (signature operand_literal 0x003e, width 2). Slide a 2-byte window over each arm's seeds, take the MIN normalized Hamming distance to 0x003e. Predict BOTH cmplog and value_profile reach distance ~0 (operand landed / climbed-to) while naive stalls. CAVEAT (scope_limit): width=2 is short -> chance-alignment prone; the rule therefore requires a real GAP vs naive (not just winner_closer), so a coincidental near-match in naive cannot pass it. CONFIRMED round 2.

hypotheses.prediction: Both resolving arms land Hamming-close to 0x003e and materially closer than naive: vp_min_distance and cmp_min_distance are near 0 while naive_min_distance is larger, giving distance_gap > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio high. DISCRIMINATING: (a) if ONLY cmplog closes and VP stays at naive's distance, the parallel claim is refuted (VP is not actually climbing the gradient — falsifies the 'both routes redundant' story). (b) A non-causal coincidence leaves winner and naive at the same distance (distance_gap ~ 0). (c) A structural/moving gate has no fixed 2-byte operand and the tool cannot score it. The 2-byte-operand chance-alignment caveat is countered by requiring the GAP, not bare closeness.
hypotheses.rule: winner_closer == true and distance_gap >= 0.25 and vp_min_distance <= 0.25 and cmp_min_distance <= 0.25
hypotheses.g2_independence: value_distance_reached computes Hamming distance from each arm's saved seeds to the gate operand; it never reads the per-trial resolve/block label. It verifies BOTH resolving corpora independently approached the operand — an independent corpus-content signal, not a re-statement of the resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 2; refuted on campaign data — kept VERBATIM for superset, labels no branch). The gate (codeInteger's true branch) is taken once PER integer literal in a densely-packed operator-joined expression body, so it is an ITERATION-DEPTH gate, not a single literal placement nor mere seed bulk. The resolving seeds drive the branch line MANY MORE times per seed than the blocking seeds. The discriminator is per-seed-normalized execution depth at the branch line.
hypotheses.novelty_vs_prior: Genuinely different from parallel_structural_accumulation_gate: that used seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count (median seed SIZE + token COUNT). This used seed_set:depth_reach (per-seed-normalized EXECUTION count of the branch line via the cov image). Against the arbiter cache the rule did NOT hold for sqlite3_14563 — the resolving seeds did not execute codeInteger's true side materially more per seed than naive in the cov image. With both this and joint_necessity refuting the two independent accumulation angles, sqlite3_14563 resolves to decidable:false (see decidable_false).

params.note: codeInteger at sqlite3.c:102982:9 true side. Kept VERBATIM for the superset/monotonic invariant; refuted against the arbiter cache (depth did not lift), so it labels no branch.

hypotheses.prediction: depth_lift >= 2.0 (resolving seeds drive codeInteger's true branch materially more times per seed than naive) AND winner_deeper == true. DISCRIMINATING: a single fixed-offset literal gate is taken at most once per seed -> depth_lift ~ 1.0; only a repeated-token iteration body lifts depth; 'resolving seeds are merely longer but execute the branch the same per-seed' -> depth_lift ~ 1.0, refuting the iteration-depth mechanism.
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach reads the cov-image per-seed execution count at the branch line for the resolving vs blocking seed sets; it never consults the per-trial resolve/block label that defined the shape.

per_branch.libxml2_6785: Gate-SHAPE limitation (unchanged from prior rounds, 4/4 rules scored, none held). The gate is `CUR == 0` at libxml2's MOVING parse cursor (the NUL byte must land at a position determined by parse state after optional 4-byte encoding detection), so there is NO FIXED input offset for any built tool to place. (1) operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) returns no_gate_signature for both arms (no fixed offset). (2) value_distance_reached scope_limits flag width<=2 operands as chance-alignment-prone (here width=1, a single NUL 0x00) AND it needs a fixed operand window to slide against — a 1-byte all-zeros 'operand' matches any low/zero byte anywhere, so a Hamming-distance gap vs naive is uninterpretable (naive seeds contain incidental zero bytes too). (3) depth_reach measures iteration depth, but CUR==0 is a single end-of-buffer test taken once, not an iteration gate. No built measurement can discriminate the parallel-NUL mechanism from coincidence for a moving 1-byte all-zero gate. Gate-SHAPE limitation (true even if libxml2 corpus were local), not corpus-availability. Honest decidable:false per G3.
per_branch.sqlite3_14563: Mechanism-vs-instrument exhaustion (FINAL round). The cards describe a per-seed accumulation / iteration-depth mechanism: codeInteger's true branch taken ~32x per winning seed, driven by a densely operator-joined integer-literal SELECT body (digits 0x35, operators 0x2b/0x2f/0x2d). The TWO independent built measurements able to test that mechanism BOTH refuted it on campaign data against the arbiter cache: (1) joint_necessity (seed_set:struct_size_and_token_count) — token_lift clipped at the small-n_W noise floor; (2) depth_reach (seed_set:depth_reach) — the resolving seeds did not execute the branch line materially more per seed than naive in the cov image. The remaining untried tools do not yield a genuinely-different discriminable mechanism: token_count (seed_set:token_count) re-measures the same integer-token accumulation that joint_necessity's token arm already exercised — a disguised relaxed re-test of an already-refuted accumulation claim (forbidden by the round rule); corpus_size_ratio (corpus_queue:corpus_size_ratio) measures corpus-POPULATION growth / energy share, which is not the per-seed structural property the mechanism asserts, so it does not fit the hypothesis. With both independent accumulation angles refuted and no genuinely-different discriminable mechanism remaining, sqlite3_14563 is honest decidable:false per G3 — no mechanism was invented to force a label. This is a measurement-discriminability limitation, NOT corpus-availability (sqlite3 corpus is local to s4).

decidable_false.reason: See per_branch. Both remaining branches resolve to honest decidable:false: libxml2_6785 by gate-SHAPE (moving 1-byte all-zero cursor gate, no built tool can place/discriminate); sqlite3_14563 by instrument exhaustion (its accumulation/iteration mechanism refuted by the two independent built measures that can test it, with no genuinely-different discriminable mechanism left). No mechanism invented to force a label.

data_realities_respected.corpus_availability: All kept measurements (operand_enrichment, joint_necessity, value_distance_reached, depth_reach) need on-disk corpus / resolving+blocking seeds. Per data_realities on-disk corpora exist for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3 (s4) and lcms/libxml2/libpng/bloaty (sB). The 7 confirmed branches were arbitrated by their owning servers (libpng+bloaty on sB). The 2 decidable:false branches are NOT marked so for corpus-availability reasons: libxml2_6785 is a gate-SHAPE limitation and sqlite3_14563 (local to s4) is a measurement-discriminability limitation — both hold regardless of which server owns the corpus. Cross-server rule respected: no branch marked decidable:false merely because its corpus is off this server.
data_realities_respected.i2s_untagged: No descriptor relies on a tagged 'I2S event' in seed_lineage. All measurements are corpus-content / corpus-reach (byte enrichment, seed size+token, Hamming distance-to-operand, per-seed execution depth) — the only feasible way to observe I2S and VP per data_realities.
data_realities_respected.value_profile_is_feedback_not_mutator: VP is measured only via what its corpus reaches: byte enrichment, seed size/tokens, Hamming-distance closure, branch-line execution depth — never via a lineage 'gradient event' (which does not exist).
data_realities_respected.byte_alignment_only_for_fixed_gates: byte_freq_ratio applies ONLY to fixed-offset value gates (the 6 confirmed libpng enum/FOURCC branches). The 2-byte bloaty enum uses value_distance_reached (Hamming closure). The structural sqlite3 branch uses seed_set tools (struct_size+token / depth_reach), never byte alignment; both refuted -> decidable:false. The moving-cursor libxml2 branch is decidable:false.

notes.priors_kept: ALL FOUR prior hypotheses retained VERBATIM (rules unchanged): parallel_fixed_offset_literal_gate (operand_enrichment) re-validates the 6 frozen libpng branches; parallel_hamming_gradient_value_closure (value_distance_reached) re-validates the confirmed bloaty_57; parallel_structural_accumulation_gate (joint_necessity) and parallel_literal_iteration_depth_gate (depth_reach) are carried forward over sqlite3_14563 for the superset/monotonic invariant even though both refuted on campaign data. Superset/monotonic invariant satisfied — no confirmed branch can regress.
notes.new_mechanisms: NONE this round. The two genuinely-different pivots proposed in round 2 (parallel_hamming_gradient_value_closure for bloaty_57; parallel_literal_iteration_depth_gate for sqlite3_14563) were already scored: the first CONFIRMED bloaty_57 (now frozen), the second REFUTED for sqlite3_14563. With its two independent accumulation measures (joint_necessity + depth_reach) both refuting and no genuinely-different discriminable mechanism remaining, proposing a third (token_count density / corpus_size_ratio) would be either a disguised relaxed re-test of the same accumulation claim or a mechanism-mismatch — neither is admissible. The honest FINAL-round outcome is decidable:false for the last unlabeled structural branch.
notes.decidable_false: TWO branches: libxml2_6785 (gate-SHAPE limitation — moving 1-byte all-zero cursor gate) and sqlite3_14563 (instrument-exhaustion — accumulation/iteration mechanism refuted by both built measures that can test it; no genuinely-different discriminable mechanism left). Both are honest inconclusive per G3, not corpus-availability, and not chasing 100%.
notes.loop_until_dry: This shape is now DRY: 7/9 confirmed-frozen, 2/9 decidable:false with a documented mechanism-vs-instrument or gate-shape rationale. No further genuinely-different discriminable mechanism exists for the remaining branches; no relaxed-threshold re-test of a failed measurement is admissible. Stop.
notes.discrimination_summary: decision_order tries H1 (fixed-offset byte enrichment) -> H3 (Hamming-distance closure) -> H2 (seed size/token) -> H4 (per-seed iteration depth). A branch validates under the FIRST rule that holds; membership lists are documentation, the arbiter applies all rules per branch in order, then falls to decidable:false / fallback for the 2 remaining branches.
